title: Why charge sheet in Pandit Sarla Bhat’s killing a milestone for J&K police? | Explained
content: The killing of the 27-year-old Pandit nurse was among the first cases of targeted killing of Kashmiri Pandits in the Valley following the armed insurrection in 1989; the filing of the charge sheet is seen by the family and the entire Pandit community as a step towards justice

title: The case for equitable development in Tamil Nadu should apply to airport too: Poovulagin Nanbargal
content: Supporters of the Parandur airport argue that if Chennai does not get a second airport by 2028, the city and its surrounding industrial regions may lose future growth opportunities

title: Safe footpath campaign in Bengaluru receives an unexpected Norwegian push
content: A group of senior officials from the Norwegian Embassy undertake a run down the footpaths of Bengaluru, shares a video extolling the experience

title: Shortage of leukocyte filter sets continues to haunt thalassemia patients in Kozhikode
content: The supply of leukocyte filter sets under the ‘Ashadhara’ scheme was stopped last year after patients complained of adverse reactions

title: Bengaluru college student alleges private photos leaked online, threatened with AI deepfake videos
content: She stated that the photographs had been shared in confidence with modelling agents between 2023 and 2024 when she was pursuing modelling opportunities, but they were never intended for publication or circulation

title: Four Nepalese nationals arrested after street brawl outside Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru goes viral
content: A verbal altercation broke out between two groups after one group allegedly objected to the other causing a disturbance during a photo session

title: Bengaluru: FIR against crèche staff over claims of physical abuse and cruelty towards children
content: The alleged incidents took place on June 25 at the ‘Little Buds Day Care’ facility located within the Capgemini campus at Brookefield

title: Registration not enough to validate Hindu marriage if rituals, ceremonies not performed: Gujarat HC
content: Ceremonies like saptapadi provide a marriage with the spiritual, social, and legal status as a sacrament and samskara, the court said

title: Madras High Court dismisses Savukku Shankar’s plea for a CBI probe against Arun IPS
content: Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan say court’s jurisdiction must not be abused by individuals who pretend to fight for justice while acting on personal or hidden motives

title: Validity of law imposing restrictions on alienation of private school properties challenged before Madras High Court
content: Chief Justice’s Bench admits the plea challenging Section 30 of the Tamil Nadu Private Schools (Regulation) Act, 2019 and seeks State government’s response

title: Iran invites Congress president Kharge, former EAM Salman Khurshid, Pawan Khera for Khamenei funeral
content: The Indian delegation will be among a large group of foreign dignitaries that have been invited by Tehran for the burial-related events, which will start from July 3 and will continue till July 9

title: AIADMK’s rebels express unwillingness to accept new positions in the party
content: Asked whether they would switch over to the TVK or the DMK, they emphatically said they would not leave the organisation, and clarified that they were not against the party leadership

title: Odisha to spend ₹5,467 crore for free education from KG to PG over next five years
content: Aimed at making quality education more accessible, equitable and affordable for students, the scheme says eligible students admitted to regular mode courses in government and government-aided institutions will be provided complete waiver of admission fees.

title: Census 2027: Enumerators begin door-to-door visits for Houselisting phase in Kerala
content: More than 61,000 enumerators, mostly teachers and other government employees, have been deployed for the exercise. The State has been divided into houselisting blocks for data collection

title: Demography panel to study population changes post 2011 Census, prepares detailed questionnaire for States
content: The High-Level Committee on Demographic Changes will seek details of population changes and increase in settlements since the 2011 Census; it will also ask the ECI for data on SIR deletions

title: Stretching ‘information’ to include Telegram
content: By reading entire platforms run by companies, which were legal entities by their own nature, under the ambit of ‘information’ under the Act, the government has effectively weaponised the content-blocking provision of Section 69A

title: Kerala HC initiates suo motu PIL into tree-felling on CHR land in Idukki
content: Allegations of large-scale tree-felling and unauthorised construction on 296 acres of cardamom title land on Gudanpara Estate, Idukki, falling under the Ponmudi Forest Section, Devikulam Range

title: VB-GRAM G workers to get a minimum of ₹300 per day as wage rates notified by Centre
content: Highest wage rate of ₹409/day in Haryana; 21 States/UTs with lower wage rates under MGNREGA see significant hikes to meet new VB-GRAM G floor rate; southern States with higher wages see minimal hikes

title: Finance Bill passed in Kerala Assembly amid Opposition boycott
content: Bill says the tax revision pertaining to low-alcohol beverages will take effect only after a notification by the government in the official gazette. LDF objects to the Bill on the grounds that UDF government is passing it without referring it to the House’s Subject Committee

title: U.P. BJP tries to shore up women voter numbers with SIR leaving wide gender gap
content: In many districts, women who have married into districts other than their own still find that they have not been added to either their native district or that of their in-laws, said a source from the BJP

title: Two Bangladeshi nationals detained at Guwahati airport
content: Security personnel allegedly recovered forged Aadhaar cards and other doubtful documents from the duo

title: Maharashtra govt. tables Women Farmers’ Empowerment Bill in Assembly
content: Under this Bill, a woman farmer will be issued a ‘Woman Farmer Certificate’. It will be her official identity document for access to government schemes, subsidies, institutional finance and market support

title: Implementation of VB-G RAM G begins in West Bengal
content: The PBKMS agricultural workers’ union, has pointed out that the allocation for VB-G RAM G scheme is less than half of what is required for the State

title: Kerala Governor gives assent to Seshadrinathan’s appointment as State Election Commissioner
content: The government had come under fire from within the Congress and also the Communist Party of India (Marxist) for nominating an alleged “Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh (RSS) fellow traveller” to the post

title: Disabled inmates must be allowed to self-identify: plea
content: The submissions recommend that prison records must identify every person with disability individually to make reasonable adjustments for them, while respecting their confidentiality

title: Over 99% of births, deaths registered in 2024: report
content: Sex ratio at birth is improving in some places, but progress remains uneven across States and Union Territories; Kerala, Arunachal Pradesh, Andaman & Nicobar Islands among top performers

title: Officials count houses, draw maps as Census fieldwork gets under way in Ernakulam
content: Enumerators begin boundary mapping and building numbering; household data collection to begin in the next phase; personal information collected to remain strictly confidential

title: Day 1 of IIIT junction diversions forces over 10,000 IT employees onto longer routes, triggers congestion
content: Closure of one access route to DLF Road No.1 forces TCS and DLF employees onto the Radisson Hotel route, increasing pressure on the Gachibowli-Miyapur corridor

title: Manipur on the boil again as 20 houses set on fire
content: Kuki and Naga organisations accuse armed groups from each other’s communities of torching villages along the India-Myanmar border in Kamjong district

title: New leaders assume key senior roles in Army and Air Force
content: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit becomes IAF Vice-Chief; Lt. Gen. Sandeep Jain assumes charge as the Vice-Chief of the Army Staff

title: Jantar Mantar turns hunger strike junction
content: The triangle of strikers have a common demand, the resignation of the Union Education Minister; activist Sonam Wangchuk is on the CJP stage; six CPI(ML) students and nine unaffiliated protesters sit on either side

title: Roche selects Hyderabad to set up digital tech hub
content: Swiss healthcare major is expanding its global tech hub network in India to Hyderabad alongside its existing site in Pune

title: 'Optional, protected, not searchable': WhatsApp answers FAQs on 'Username' feature
content: WhatsApp is clarifying its upcoming username feature amidst government concerns about potential fraud and impersonation. The company assures users usernames are optional and will not be publicly searchable.  Existing popular names are reserved for legitimate owners.  However, the Indian government has asked Meta to halt the rollout until consultations are complete, citing risks of scams and identity spoofing.

title: Why Iran fears another funeral disaster as Khamenei awaits burial for 4 months
content: Iran is meticulously planning Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's funeral to prevent the deadly crowd surges that marred previous burials. Lessons learned from the chaotic funerals of Ayatollah Khomeini and General Qasem Soleimani are shaping extensive security measures, including potential helicopter deployment and cross-border coordination with Iraq. The aim is to ensure Khamenei's final rites are a display of state control, not a repeat of past tragedies.

title: Watch: 'Kya kar raha hai, yaar?' — Abhishek frustrated after Kishan mix-up
content: Ishan Kishan suffered a second successive run-out dismissal after a mix-up with Abhishek Sharma in India's first T20I against England. A frustrated Abhishek was heard saying, "Tu pahuncha ki nahin pahuncha, kya kar raha hai yaar Ishan?" However, Abhishek recovered brilliantly with a 24-ball 59 as India posted 189/7. Persistent rain later forced the match to be abandoned.

title: Days after Meta Layoffs, Zuckerberg says companies should empower workers, not replace them
content: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg believes AI-driven job losses are exaggerated, advocating for tools that boost individual productivity. This comes as the tech giant laid off 8,000 employees, reassigning another 7,000 to AI projects, to offset significant investments in its AI "reboot." While Meta pours billions into AI infrastructure, Zuckerberg suggests a focus on human empowerment could create more jobs, a view contrasting with some industry leaders.

title: India condemns demolition of 125-year-old gurdwara in Pakistan
content: India has strongly condemned the reported demolition of a 125-year-old Sikh Gurdwara in Pakistan's Farooqabad, terming it a "targeted act of vandalism." The Ministry of External Affairs expressed grave concern over the lack of action by local authorities and the Evacuee Trust Property Board. This incident is seen as part of a continuing pattern of attacks on religious minorities and their places of worship in Pakistan.

title: Lukaku plays peacemaker as Belgium teammates clash before stunning comeback vs Senegal
content: Belgium staged a sensational World Cup comeback against Senegal, winning 3-2 in a dramatic Round of 32 clash. Trailing 0-2 until the 86th minute, striker Romelu Lukaku first diffused an on-field spat between teammates before igniting the revival.  He scored, then Trossard assisted Tielemans for the equaliser, who later netted a record-breaking 125th-minute penalty to seal the victory after a lengthy VAR review.

title: Watch: China unveils truck-mounted EMALS that fires drones into sky in seconds
content: China has unveiled a truck-mounted electromagnetic aircraft launch system, enabling fixed-wing drones to take flight without traditional runways. This mobile platform, adaptable for various terrains, utilizes advanced technology to rapidly accelerate drones, enhancing the People's Liberation Army's drone capabilities. The system's rapid deployment and maneuverability offer increased resilience and flexibility for drone operations in modern conflict zones.

title: Sergey Brin sold his NYC real estate: Here's what Mamdani's rent freeze cost him
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin has divested his stake in a New York City real estate fund for a fraction of its value, selling for just six cents on the dollar. This significant loss comes as Mayor Zohran Mamdani's rent freeze tightens conditions for landlords. The fund, A&E Real Estate, confirmed the sale, citing the challenging market for rent-stabilized apartments.

title: Delhi HC spares husband, lawyers over wife's intimate photos, flags dignity breach
content: The Delhi High Court declined to initiate contempt proceedings against a husband and his lawyers for unredacted intimate photos in a divorce petition, deeming it a "grave lapse." While accepting an apology and ordering the photos sealed, the court stressed the importance of protecting personal dignity in matrimonial disputes, warning against using private material as weapons. The wife's identity will now be anonymized.

title: Watch: Tillman's stunning free-kick after Balogun's red card sends USA into last 16
content: Malik Tillman's spectacular free-kick secured a dramatic 2-0 World Cup Round of 16 berth for the USA against Bosnia and Herzegovina. Despite Folarin Balogun's controversial red card, Tillman's late strike, even after a boot change for a bloody toe, sealed the historic win. Captain Christian Pulisic lauded the team's fighting spirit, while coach Mauricio Pochettino questioned the red card. The US face Belgium next, aiming for their second-ever knockout win.

title: Microsoft plans another round of layoffs, may cut thousands of jobs across teams in ...
content: Microsoft is gearing up for another round of job cuts, reportedly impacting sales, consulting, and Xbox divisions. While smaller than previous reductions, thousands of positions may be affected. This move comes as the tech giant prioritizes AI investments and cost control, even as its stock has seen a dip.  A voluntary retirement program earlier this year also aimed to manage headcount.

title: Row over Vance saying his Catholic faith makes him opposed to 'low-wage foreigners'
content: US Vice President JD Vance has stirred debate with his remarks connecting his Catholic beliefs to his stance against 'low-wage foreigners' in America. His comments, which emphasize integrity in Christian teachings, faced backlash from critics, including those pointing out his Hindu wife, Usha Vance. As discussions unfold in the MAGA movement, Vance defends the position that his faith drives policies aimed at preserving American dignity rather than merely benefiting corporations.

title: Stolen cash hidden in Ram Mandir bathroom before being smuggled out in small tranches
content: Ayodhya police are intensifying their probe into alleged Ram Temple donation embezzlement. Arrested accused Avinash Shukla reportedly revealed a scheme where cash was hidden in temple washrooms before being smuggled out. Investigators are examining CCTV blind spots, key access to the counting room, and financial trails, including alleged investments in property. Recoveries so far total over Rs 79 lakh in cash and valuables.

title: Unable to withdraw PF or file claims? EPFO services down for 7 days
content: Employees' Provident Fund Organisation (EPFO) services remain unavailable beyond the scheduled July 1 deadline due to a planned database consolidation and software upgrade. This outage, affecting member and employer portals, prevents access to crucial functions like claim submission and e-passbook. Meanwhile, a new EPF Scheme, 2026, has been notified, introducing digital reforms and simplified withdrawal rules, alongside initiatives to address past compliance issues.

title: 8 killed in Russia's ballistic missile, drone attack on Kyiv
content: Kyiv endured a brutal overnight assault by Russia, with drones and ballistic missiles claiming at least eight lives and injuring many. Explosions rocked the capital, damaging residential buildings and a hotel. This escalation follows Ukraine's recent long-range drone strikes on Russian territory, highlighting the evolving role of unmanned systems in the ongoing conflict.

title: 'I know their names': RG Kar victim's mother says all involved 'will be caught & punished'
content: The mother of the RG Kar Medical College and Hospital rape and murder victim, BJP MLA Ratna Debnath, has declared she knows the identities of those responsible for her daughter's death and the alleged cover-up. She vowed that all culprits, including those who concealed evidence, will face justice. This statement follows a Calcutta High Court order for a new Special Investigation Team, citing dissatisfaction with the previous probe.

title: 'It will put Sooryavanshi under more pressure': Gavaskar warns Team India
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's India debut wait continued after he was left out of the first T20I against England despite his stellar IPL season. Sunil Gavaskar said the delay could increase pressure but felt the teenager would handle expectations. If selected later, Sooryavanshi could become India's youngest men's international cricketer and the youngest player in top-level men's T20s if given an opportunity.

title: Rs 56L loan, AI accomplice, affair: How Bengaluru techie planned triple murder
content: Bengaluru police are investigating a chilling triple murder where a techie's live-in partner allegedly used AI for guidance on fatal stab wounds. The couple is accused of killing her parents and sister over mounting loan debts.  The partner confessed to the killings while the woman watched, with the motive reportedly stemming from financial pressures and infidelity.

title: Monsoon onset brings relief: Heavy rain lashes parts of Delhi-NCR
content: Delhi-NCR experienced a welcome respite from intense heat as rain lashed parts of the region on Thursday, with the IMD issuing a red alert for storms.  Following cloudy skies and light showers on Wednesday, the southwest monsoon is advancing, promising more rain and pleasant weather for the next four to five days.  Expect thunderstorms, lightning, and gusty winds as conditions become favourable for the monsoon's official arrival.

title: Lions linked with Cowboys CB as Detroit eyes secondary reinforcements
content: The Detroit Lions could explore a trade with the Dallas Cowboys after releasing former first-round cornerback Terrion Arnold following his legal issues. Dallas has strong depth at cornerback, making Caelen Carson a potential trade candidate if the team stays healthy through training camp. While no deal is imminent, Carson's rookie contract and promising performances make him an affordable option for a Lions secondary that suddenly needs reinforcements before Week 1.

title: Chargers, Panthers emerge as favorites for Commanders star Daron Payne
content: Daron Payne's future with the Commanders hangs in the balance as he enters the final year of his substantial contract. With a new coaching staff and defensive scheme, his 2026 performance will be crucial. Several teams, including the Chargers, Jaguars, Panthers, and Lions, are reportedly monitoring his situation and could pursue him if he hits free agency next spring, seeking to bolster their defensive lines.

title: Rangers land Durzi, Beaudoin in blockbuster Vincent Trocheck trade
content: Vincent Trocheck is set to join the Utah Mammoth in a deal that will send Sean Durzi, prospect Cole Beaudoin, and a 2027 third-round pick to the Rangers. The move is part of New York's ongoing roster retool under GM Chris Drury. Trocheck leaves after several successful seasons, while the Rangers add both immediate defensive help and future talent.

title: SRK unveils LA Knight Riders stadium as cricket gears up for Olympics 2028
content: Shah Rukh Khan unveiled the LA Knight Riders Cricket Ground in Pomona, California, marking a major step for cricket's growth in the United States. The stadium will host Major League Cricket matches and is expected to play a key role in the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics. Calling it a "dream" turned reality, Shah Rukh said the venue is meant for sports, entertainment, and creating lasting memories.

title: Stock recommendations for today - check list
content: Top stock market recommendations: REC, Oberoi Realty, and Home First Finance Company India have been recommended by Aakash K Hindocha, Vice President - Research, Nuvama Professional Clients Group/Nuvama Wealth Management as top stocks to buy on July 2, 2026. He has also shared his views on Nifty and Bank Nifty.

title: Watch: 9-storey Kyiv apartment building partially collapses after Russian attack
content: Kyiv endured a devastating overnight missile and drone assault, with a residential building partially collapsing and multiple fires erupting. This follows Ukraine's own long-range strikes on Russian targets. The escalating conflict sees both sides intensifying attacks, as casualty figures in the war reportedly surpass two million. Russia, meanwhile, reiterates warnings against continued US military aid to Ukraine.

title: Delhi woman loses Wipro job after 5.5 years, her response has the internet talking
content: A Delhi marketing professional, Anukriti Vidyarthi, shared her unexpected layoff from Wipro after five-and-a-half years.  Describing the experience as a potential 'sign from God,' she expressed the personal impact of losing job security. Her candid account on social media resonated with many, sparking conversations about corporate layoffs and the shared anxieties of professionals facing similar uncertainties.

title: Degrees of denial: Paris blames US for climate change in heated exchange
content: A transatlantic debate ignites as Paris blames America's heavy air conditioning use for contributing to Europe's heatwave deaths. While the US mocks France's lack of AC, experts highlight America's significant emissions. The article contrasts this with the US's own struggles with extreme heat and a dismissive attitude towards climate science, underscoring the urgent need for sustainable cooling solutions.

title: Will Balogun miss USA's Round of 16 clash vs Belgium after red card? Rules explained
content: USA secured a World Cup Round of 16 spot with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina, but star striker Folarin Balogun was controversially red-carded. His dismissal, following a VAR review for stepping on an opponent's ankle, means he's suspended for the Belgium clash unless an appeal succeeds. Balogun, who scored his third tournament goal before the incident, remains a key player for the US.

title: World’s oceans break June heat record: Monitor
content: Oceans worldwide recorded their hottest June ever, with global average sea surface temperatures reaching 20.98°C. Scientists warn that the developing El Nino phenomenon, coupled with ongoing climate change, could push ocean and atmospheric heat to new peaks in the coming months and into next year. This alarming trend highlights the escalating impact of global warming on marine environments.

title: Critical minerals to semiconductor: What's on the agenda as Japan PM reaches India
content: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is in New Delhi for a three-day visit, focusing on the 16th India-Japan Annual Summit. Discussions will center on deepening cooperation in economic security, resilient supply chains, emerging technologies, and defence. Both nations aim to bolster their Special Strategic and Global Partnership, with a strong emphasis on investment, innovation, and industrial collaboration amidst evolving global dynamics.

title: 'Sprayed toilet jet water into mouths': Toddlers abused for crying in Bengaluru
content: Horrifying allegations have surfaced against five nannies at a Capgemini campus day care in Bengaluru.  Children were reportedly subjected to cruel treatment, including being locked in bathrooms and placed inside a washing machine.  A child helpline official filed a complaint after receiving disturbing video evidence, leading to police registering a case. Capgemini is cooperating with authorities and has temporarily closed the facility.

title: Earthquake of magnitude 4.7 strikes Tajikistan; no immediate reports of damage
content: Tajikistan experienced a 4.7 magnitude earthquake early Thursday morning, with tremors felt at a depth of 122 km. This follows a 4.0 magnitude quake on June 21. The nation, known for its mountainous terrain, is highly susceptible to seismic activity. Climate change is exacerbating existing vulnerabilities, posing increased risks to infrastructure and remote communities.

title: Venezuela quake survivors seek food, shelter as toll rises to nearly 2,000
content: A miraculous rescue saw a three-year-old boy pulled alive from rubble six days after devastating earthquakes struck Venezuela. Tens of thousands are still desperately seeking food and shelter amidst widespread shortages and broken services. With nearly 2,000 confirmed dead and thousands missing, the nation grapples with the aftermath of one of Latin America's worst seismic disasters, facing immense humanitarian challenges and a strained economy.

title: FIFA WC: USA beat Bosnia for only their 2nd-ever knockout win despite Balogun red card
content: The United States secured a historic 2-0 win against Bosnia and Herzegovina, marking their second-ever World Cup knockout-stage victory. Despite Folarin Balogun's controversial red card, the team persevered, with Balogun scoring the opener and Malik Tillman sealing the triumph with a stunning late free-kick. This memorable performance propels the hosts into the last 16, setting up a clash with Belgium.

title: Freak accident near Atal Tunnel: Scorpio falls onto under-construction bridge, 1 dead
content: Tragedy struck near the Atal Tunnel late Monday night as a road accident claimed the life of Kailash Kumar from Rajasthan and left three others injured. Their vehicle reportedly lost control, plunging onto an under-construction cement structure. The injured, also from Rajasthan and Uttar Pradesh, are receiving medical attention. This unfortunate incident highlights the dangers of travel in the region.

title: Couple arrested after proposal atop NYC's skyscraper - watch
content: A daring stunt saw two individuals scale the Empire State Building without safety gear, unfurling a peace banner and reportedly getting engaged at the summit. Ivan Kuznetsov and Angelina Nikolau, known for such climbs, were apprehended after accessing the spire via a maintenance hatch. They face multiple charges including burglary and reckless endangerment for their audacious act.

title: Hormuz oil shock sends India back to Russia: Is this a peak or the new normal?
content: Russia has emerged as the dominant supplier of crude oil for India - with imports touching over 240 million barrels in the four months since the US-Iran conflict began. In fact, crude imports from Russia are over 4 times the nearest supplier - UAE at over 58 million barrels.

title: 'China trying to take over': Trump calls giving away Panama Canal 'stupid mistake'
content: Former President Donald Trump voiced strong disapproval of the US handover of the Panama Canal, citing increased fees and alleged Chinese influence. He vowed to prevent Beijing's takeover.  Trump also reiterated his stance on birthright citizenship, believing the Supreme Court erred, while celebrating a ruling that bolstered presidential removal powers for executive officials.

title: Džeko becomes oldest player in a WC knockout match, but record may last only a day
content: Bosnia's Edin Džeko made World Cup history as the oldest outfield player in a knockout match at 40. His landmark game ended prematurely due to injury, with the US leading 1-0 before a red card for their scorer Balogun. Despite the potential for his record to be broken soon, Džeko's achievement is cemented, as Bosnia now seeks an equalizer against a depleted US side.

title: Farooq, Mehbooba join call for India-Pak dialogue, BJP says terror and talks can't go together
content: Former J&K CMs Farooq Abdullah and Mehbooba Mufti, alongside nearly 60 Indian signatories, have urged India and Pakistan to resume dialogue for regional peace. Their letter highlights the lost opportunities for millions of young people due to ongoing hostility.  Kashmir's chief cleric also supported the call, emphasizing that engagement is the only way to resolve differences and build a prosperous South Asia.

title: Shreyas Iyer completes 5,000 international runs during captain's knock against England
content: Shreyas Iyer achieved a significant milestone, surpassing 5,000 international runs with a captain's knock of 68. He anchored India's innings after a shaky start, guiding them to 189/7. Abhishek Sharma also impressed with a rapid half-century and a T20I sixes record. Despite rain interruptions, India set a competitive target for England.

title: Amid Deepika's 8-hour shift row, Hirani says Ranbir would come on set at 3am for 'Sanju' shoot
content: Filmmaker Rajkumar Hirani lauded Ranbir Kapoor's dedication during the filming of 'Sanju,' revealing the actor arrived at 3 am daily for a grueling 4-5 hour makeup session to portray Sanjay Dutt. Despite 12-hour shoots, Kapoor never complained, showcasing a commitment that contrasts with current discussions about shorter work shifts in the industry. Hirani emphasized that committed actors understand the demands of finishing a project.

title: Census 2027: Over 74,000 self-enumeration submissions received in Kozhikode
content: Kerala’s self-enumeration window closed on June 30,2026. Field enumeration will begin after boundary mapping and house numbering are completed. Enumerators to verify self-reported data

title: Arunachal clan launches drive to conserve endangered ray-finned fish, sustain tradition
content: Members of the Sangno clan translocate 52 fingerlings of the Schizothorax pelzami from one stream in East Kameng district to another, where they will be relatively safer from the predatory mahseer

title: Despite relief, hotels unlikely to reduce food prices soon; owners want LPG at pre-war rates
content: Restaurateurs welcome the price cut, but say it will make little difference; they want the OMCs to roll back the prices to around ₹2,000 by the end of July

title: Chennai Corporation to privatise operations of 6 Padaipagams
content: The facilities are located at Jawahar Nagar, Jaganathan Street, Kanigapuram, Otteri, Mangalapuram, and Pulianthope in north Chennai

title: Fall of trees not in human hands: Minister Sanjay Shirsat under fire for remarks on Mumbai school bus tragedy
content: Slamming the Shiv Sena leader, Congress’ Nana Patole said the remarks show "the level of arrogance" within the BJP-led Mahayuti government

title: Umbrella atop chariot of Sri Narasimhar tilts during procession
content: Official sources in the temple said that it was a minor incident and that there was a slightly strong wind, which caused the umbrella to lose balance

title: IMD issues red alert for coastal Karnataka on July 2 and 3
content: Karnataka State Natural Disaster Monitoring Centre (KSNDMC) stated that coastal Karnataka recorded 53 mm rainfall in 24 hours ending at 8.30 a.m. on July 1 against the normal rainfall of 41 mm for the same period

title: Pak Businessman Demolishes 125-Year-Old Gurdwara, India Calls It "Highly Deplorable"
content: Foreign Ministry spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal urged Islamabad to investigate the incident and restore the damaged portions of the religious structure. New Delhi also called on Islamabad to ensure the safety and well-being of religious minorities.

title: India-EU Trade Pack Likely To Be Implemented By Early 2027: Envoy To NDTV
content: Asked why a deal that has been negotiated on and off for 19 years was finally crossing the line now, the ambassador pushed back on the idea that global tariff turmoil forced India and the EU together.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Markets Likely To Open In Green As Crude Oil Eases Further
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Brent crude falls 1.09% to $70.79 while US WTI falls 1.27% to $67.71 per barrel.

title: 2 Killed, 11 Injured As Russia Launches Massive Drone Attack On Ukraine
content: The attack came after Ukraine's air force warned that ballistic missiles were headed towards the capital and followed Zelensky cutting short a visit to Dublin Wednesday

title: Weather LIVE Updates: Monsoon Onset Brings Relief, Rain In Parts Of Delhi
content: Weather Today, Delhi-NCR Rain, Monsoon Live Updates: Delhi-NCR sees rain and temperature drop as IMD issues yellow alert for monsoon activity.

title: Dhurandhar Showed The Gangsters. Lyari Wants The World To See Its Football
content: When Brazil scored a stoppage time winner against Japan at the World Cup, thousands of miles away, in the narrow lanes of Lyari in Pakistan's Karachi, there was something closer to catharsis.

title: Vijay Moves Supreme Court Against Cow Slaughter Ban, Sparks Political Row
content: The Madras High Court on May 27 imposed a blanket ban on the slaughter of cows and calves in Tamil Nadu.

title: Three Mega Shifts That Could Decide India's Next Decade of Jobs
content: For Indian businesses, investors and professionals, the biggest challenge may no longer be predicting the next market cycle.

title: India-Japan Summit 2026 LIVE: Trade, Technology, Defence In Focus At NDTV Dialogue
content: India-Japan Summit 2026 LIVE News Updates: The event today will feature prominent voices including Union Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal, Daikin India Chairman and Managing Director Kanwal Jeet Jawa, India's Ambassador to Japan Nagma Mallick and many others

title: Economic Security Declaration To AI: What's On Japan PM's India Agenda
content: Artificial intelligence would be a specific subject for a separate joint statement between the two sides, who also plan to collaborate on research and development, industrial applications, digital public infrastructure, skill development and responsible governance of technologies.

title: Why Delhi's New EV Policy Aims Two And Three Wheelers
content: The new policy proposes to stop the registration of new petrol-powered two-wheelers from April 2028 and restrict new registration of CNG-powered three-wheelers from the end of this year.

title: US In Talks With AI Companies For Voluntary Model Standards: Report
content: The US government is in advanced talks with AI companies to create voluntary standards for the release of new models, with an announcement possible as soon as next week, the Financial Times reported on Wednesday, citing sources.

title: Qatar Says US-Iran Doha Talks Make "Positive Progress" On 14-Point Deal
content: Qatar and Pakistan have concluded separate meetings with US and Iranian negotiators in Doha, with "positive progress" reported on issues related to the 14-point Memorandum of Understanding (MoU), according to Qatar's foreign ministry official spokesperson.

title: 13 kg Opium, Rs 30 Lakh Drug-Linked Property Seized In Haryana; 2 Arrested
content: A search of the vehicle allegedly led to the recovery of 13.06 kilograms of opium, estimated to have an illicit market value of nearly Rs 70 lakh.

title: Rs 700-Crore Delhi Medical Scam: Two Accused Sent To Judicial Custody
content: According to the officer, the investigation remains at an early stage and further action will depend entirely on evidence collected during the probe.

title: "Getting Along Very Well": Trump Sees Progress As US, Iran Hold Qatar Talks
content: US President Donald Trump said Wednesday that indirect talks with Iran in Qatar were making progress, offering a tentative sign that diplomacy was holding after recent exchanges of fire threatened efforts to end the Middle East war.

title: 'Vagabond' Who Changed The World: Einstein's Journey From Failure To Fame
content: Einstein enrolled at ETH Zurich in October 1896 at the age of just 17. Even then, he was different from his fellow students. Decades later, he reflected on his university years with remarkable honesty.

title: Govt asks WhatsApp to pause username rollout
content: new feature would allow first-time contact through usernames alone . users would also have the option of protecting their account with a "username key" the move could "materially increase" incidents of online fraud, phishing, digital arrest scams and impersonation .

title: 'Our chats being monitored': Dipke alleges phone surveillance as protest enters day 13
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most." title: Bengaluru cab explosion: Jilted lover had bought crude bombs for Rs 600
content: jilted lover bought crude bombs for 600 from karnataka's Honnavar . he triggered an explosion in a cab in Tumakuru district leaving him dead . man from Uttara kannada district has come under the police scanner .
